addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- codebase-design: Shared vocabulary for designing deep modules. Use when the user wants to design or improve a module's interface, find deepening opportunities, decide where a seam goes, make code more testable or AI-navigable, or when another skill needs the deep-module vocabulary.
- diagnosing-bugs: Diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Use when the user says "diagnose"/"debug this", or reports something broken/throwing/failing/slow.
- domain-modeling: Build and sharpen a project's domain model. Use when the user wants to pin down domain terminology or a ubiquitous language, record an architectural decision, or when another skill needs to maintain the domain model.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- grilling: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design. Use when the user wants to stress-test a plan before building, or uses any 'grill' trigger phrases.
- tdd: Test-driven development. Use when the user wants to build features or fix bugs test-first, mentions "red-green-refactor", or wants integration tests.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

{"metadata":{"type":"page"},"title":"JD","url":"https://app.notion.com/p/388030966f8d80b48145c3b70b127af8","text":"Here is the result of "view" for the Page with URL https://app.notion.com/p/388030966f8d80b48145c3b70b127af8 as of 2026-06-23T04:17:37.181Z: {"title":"JD"} A Full-Stack developer with an exceptional command on Python and React.js - Minimun experience of 4+ years in a growth-stage or early-stage venture will be preferred. - Dreamer, a tech architect and a hands-on developer all rolled into one - Working knowledge of building a highly scalable application - Responsible for building the engineering stack that is the foundation for our products and services 1 Aviate is solving pressing problems to unlock a multi-billion dollar opportunity and a chance to transform the recruitment and job-preparation markets in India. We are creating a suite of recruitment products for job seekers and employers. We currently operate Aviate, our B2C employability enhancement and placement product for job-seekers and Q-Rate, our B2B SaaS play targeted at large employers and recruitment agencies. - \*Learn More about Aviate: [www.aviate.jobs](http://www.aviate.jobs/) **Learn More about Q-Rate: **[**www.qrate.ai**](http://www.qrate.ai/) We are a profitably growing venture with over 200 clients and have been backed by the Quant fund of AngelList and some prominent entrepreneurs of the Indian startup ecosystem including founders of companies like Khatabook, Lets Transport and Housing.com. We are seeking a dexterous Full-Stack Engineer with the following attributes. - A Full-Stack developer with an exceptional command on Python and React.js - Minimun experience of 4+ years in a growth-stage or early-stage venture will be preferred. - Dreamer, a tech architect and a hands-on developer all rolled into one - Working knowledge of building a highly scalable application - Responsible for building the engineering stack that is the foundation for our products and services If you feel you fit the bill, please read along for the key responsibilities that the role would entail: Key responsibilities - Write effective, scalable code - Building a tech tream in collaboration with the founders - Develop back-end components to improve responsiveness and overall performance - Integrate user-facing elements into applications - Test and debug programs - Improve functionality of existing systems - Implement security and data protection solutions - Assess and prioritize feature requests - Coordinate with internal teams to understand user requirements and provide technical solutions 2 What you will do
• Work on internal tools and dashboards in React JS used by teams across the company.
• Contribute to Python services that power internal APIs, job orchestration and workflow automation.
• Deploy AI agents that automate repetitive internal workflows like ETL pipelines and configuration management.
• Build log-driven automations that detect known issues and apply known fixes without manual intervention.
• Refactor legacy platform code using Claude Code and Cursor, turning years of accumulated complexity into clean foundations.
• Partner with engineering teams to improve developer productivity and shipping velocity. What we are looking for
• 2+ years building production systems in Python.
• 2+ years building production React JS interfaces with TypeScript, modern hooks patterns and state management.
• Systems thinking with experience in queues, async pipelines and service-to-service contracts.
• Track record of working in legacy codebases using Claude or similar AI tools to ship confidently.
• Daily user of AI coding tools with a clear philosophy on how to apply them.
• Comfortable contributing to developer-facing surfaces where the UX bar is high. Bonus
• Built and deployed AI agents in production, ideally with tool use, memory or multi-step reasoning.
• Experience working with open-source LLMs in production.
• LLM orchestration frameworks, eval pipelines or agent observability.
• ETL automation experience.
• Contributions to internal developer platforms. 3 **The Role** We're hiring our founding engineer and technical lead the senior-most member of engineering and the person who owns this platform end to end: frontend, backend, infrastructure, and the AI agent layer. You won't be a cog in a team. You'll be the technical backbone. You'll work shoulder-to-shoulder with the founders to turn vision into architecture, set the engineering bar, and direct a fleet of AI coding agents that you scope, review, and ship with. As the company grows, you'll define how we build, write the standards others follow, and bring up the engineers who join behind you. You should be the kind of engineer who treats Claude and GPT as force multipliers, not novelties — someone who can drive multiple AI agents in parallel and still hold an uncompromising line on architecture, security, and code quality. **Why This Role** - Founding ownership of something real. Not a greenfield experiment a substantial, production-grade codebase with customers depending on it. Your job is to take it further, faster. You set the foundation. Architecture, standards, tooling, hiring bar, engineering culture the decisions you make become the ground everyone else builds on. AI-augmented from day one. You'll ship with a workflow most companies are still only talking about: multiple agents in parallel, human judgment on top. Direct line to the founders. No layers, no politics. You're in the room for every technical decision that matters. Equity, autonomy, and impact that scale with the company you help build.\* **What You'll Own** - The full thinQit codebase: Next.js 14 (App Router) + React 18/19 + TypeScript web app, the marketing site, and three React Native (Expo) mobile apps. The AI agent layer: two-phase code-generation agents (planner + executor), self-fixing QA automation, wireframe generation, citation-validation loops, and 90+ document AI operations across OpenAI GPT-5-series, Claude, and Google models. Multi-LLM orchestration and MCP servers: routing, fallbacks, and the cost/quality/latency tradeoffs that decide which model runs where. Azure infrastructure: Web Apps, Container Apps, Service Bus, Blob Storage, Key Vault, PostgreSQL deployed via Bicep and Terraform with multi-stage Docker builds. CI/CD 21 GitHub Actions workflows covering build, test, and deploy. The data and API layer a 47-table PostgreSQL schema, 173 API endpoints, and the multi-tenant auth and billing layer around them. Quality 300+ Vitest unit tests and Playwright E2E suites. Keep them green, grow them, and make them mean something.\* **Technical Leadership & Direction** This is a lead role, and the leadership is real even before the team is large: Set the architecture and defend it. Make the hard calls on system design, then document the why so the next engineer inherits reasoning, not just code. Define how humans and AI agents build together. You're designing the playbook for agent-augmented engineering at thinQit task scoping, review gates, what ships autonomously and what doesn't. Own the engineering bar. Code review standards, security practices, testing discipline, and the definition of "production-ready" are yours to set and enforce. Build the team. As we grow, you'll shape the hiring bar, interview and select engineers, and mentor them into the standards you've set. Be the technical voice in the room with founders, with customers when it counts, and in the tradeoff conversations between speed, cost, and quality. **Day to Day** - Ship across the stack from React UI to Express/Next.js API routes to database migrations to Azure deployment. Orchestrate multiple AI coding agents in parallel scope tasks, review output, and merge production-grade code fast without dropping the bar. Design and harden agentic workflows prompt pipelines, tool use, evaluation, and self-correction loops. Own production monitoring, incidents, performance, and cost optimization across both Azure and LLM spend. Make architecture calls and document them you're setting decisions others will build on for years. Raise the team's ceiling through reviews, standards, and the systems that make good work the default.\* **The Stack at a Glance** Frontend: Next.js 14 (App Router), React 18/19, TypeScript, React Native (Expo) Backend: Node.js, Express / Next.js API routes, 173 endpoints AI: OpenAI (GPT-5-series), Anthropic (Claude), Google multi-LLM orchestration + MCP servers Data: PostgreSQL (47-table schema), multi-tenant auth & billing Infra: Azure (Web Apps, Container Apps, Service Bus, Blob Storage, Key Vault), Docker (multi-stage), Bicep + Terraform CI/CD & QA: 21 GitHub Actions workflows, Vitest (300+ tests), Playwright E2E **What Success Looks Like** First 30 days — You're shipping to production, fluent in the codebase and deployment pipeline, and you've mapped the highest-leverage risks and opportunities across the platform. First 90 days — You're independently owning major surfaces of the stack, your agent-driven workflow is moving real features end to end, and you've made (and documented) your first foundational architecture decisions. First 6–12 months — You've measurably raised reliability, performance, and cost-efficiency; the testing and review standards you set are the team default; and you've laid the groundwork — and possibly hired — to scale engineering behind you. **Must-Haves** 5+ years full-stack engineering with deep TypeScript, React, and Node.js (Next.js App Router experience strongly preferred). Production experience building with LLMs OpenAI and/or Anthropic APIs, agentic workflows, prompt engineering, tool use, and evaluation. Beyond demos. Hands-on AI-assisted development you already use Claude Code, Codex, Copilot, or similar daily and can manage multiple agents at once. Strong Azure experience App Service / Container Apps, Service Bus, Key Vault, and IaC (Bicep or Terraform). Docker fluency, including multi-stage builds and container deployment. PostgreSQL at scale schema design, migrations, query performance. CI/CD ownership with GitHub Actions or equivalent. Founder mentality comfortable with ambiguity, ruthless prioritization, and shipping without a safety net. Ownership and judgment to lead you can hold the technical bar, make decisions others rely on, and bring people up to your standard. **Nice-to-Haves** MCP server development or LLM orchestration frameworks. React Native / Expo experience. Multi-tenant SaaS architecture and billing systems (Stripe or similar). Deep Playwright / Vitest testing practice and security hardening of AI-generated code. Experience mentoring engineers or leading a small team. **Who You Are** You ship. You'd rather have something real in production than something perfect in a doc. You hold a high bar under pressure speed doesn't make you sloppy. You think in systems and tradeoffs, and you can explain your reasoning to a founder or a junior engineer with equal clarity. You're genuinely excited that AI agents do part of the work and clear-eyed about where human judgment is non-negotiable. You want the ownership, and you're ready for the accountability that comes with it. 4 **What you'll own:** This is a true full-stack role, balanced across Go and TypeScript. You'll own features from API design and data model to the UI — and the design-driven codegen chain that keeps the contract honest in between. You'll work across three areas: - Backend services & data — Go services behind a design-driven Goa API, PostgreSQL schemas and typed sqlc queries, and event-driven workflows on Kafka. - Frontend product surfaces — Next.js / React interfaces for AR/AP, cash forecasting, reconciliation, and dashboards, consuming the API through generated, end-to-end-typed contracts. - The seams in between — multi-tenant isolation, service-to-service integration with our agentic and workflow services, money correctness, and observability across the stack. **Stack:** Golang, PostgreSQL, Message Queues, Next.js, React, Typescript, NodeJS, Docker, Terraform **You should have:** - 4+ years building and shipping production web applications end-to-end, comfortable owning both server and client. - Strong backend skills in a statically typed language — Go preferred (or strong Java / C# / Rust / TypeScript-backend experience and eagerness to go deep in Go) — building REST APIs and services. - Strong frontend skills with React and TypeScript, and a modern framework (Next.js or equivalent). - Solid relational database fundamentals — schema design, migrations, and writing and optimizing SQL (PostgreSQL preferred). - Experience with API contracts and codegen (OpenAPI, Goa, GraphQL, or similar) and keeping types honest across the boundary. - Familiarity with event-driven / async systems (Kafka, queues, or similar) and the failure modes they bring. - A habit of testing across the stack — backend unit and integration plus frontend unit and e2e. - A correctness mindset for sensitive data — you reach for transactions, idempotency, and careful validation by instinct. - Strong collaboration and written communication — thrives in a design-driven, async, written-first culture alongside PM, design, and engineering. 5 # **About the job** **Company Description:** We are building an AI-native travel platform that rethinks how people discover, plan, and experience travel. Our products combine conversational AI, intelligent personalization, and modern interface design to create seamless travel experiences that feel proactive, adaptive, and deeply personal. We move fast, care deeply about product quality, and believe exceptional UX is a competitive advantage — especially in AI products. **Role Description:** We’re looking for a strong frontend-leaning full stack engineer who can build polished, high-performance user experiences while also being comfortable working across backend systems and APIs. This is not a pure frontend implementation role. You should care deeply about interaction quality, responsiveness, and product thinking — but also understand how systems are designed end-to-end. You’ll work closely with product, design, and AI/backend teams to ship intelligent product experiences that scale. The ideal candidate has experience building consumer-facing products, strong frontend architecture instincts, and enough backend depth to independently own features across the stack. **Key Responsibilities:** *Frontend Architecture & Product Experience* - Build scalable and maintainable frontend systems using React and modern web technologies. - Create high-quality interfaces with strong attention to interaction design, motion, responsiveness, and usability. - Develop reusable component systems and patterns that enable rapid product iteration. - Collaborate closely with design to elevate overall product polish and consistency. *AI-Native User Experiences* - Design and implement interfaces for conversational and AI-assisted workflows. - Translate complex AI behaviors into intuitive, trustworthy, and understandable user experiences. - Build streaming, real-time, and stateful interactions across AI surfaces. *Full Stack Feature Ownership* - Own features end-to-end across frontend and backend layers. - Integrate APIs, manage application state, and contribute to backend architecture where needed. - Work with databases, authentication systems, caching layers, and infrastructure primitives. - Collaborate with AI/backend engineers to connect models and orchestration systems into product experiences. *Performance & Engineering Quality* - Optimize application performance across rendering, loading, caching, and network behavior. - Maintain high engineering standards through thoughtful architecture, code reviews, and testing practices. - Improve developer experience, tooling, and frontend reliability. **Qualifications:** - **Experience building a consumer app is a must. Please attach your portfolio.** - Demonstrated experience building modern UI in production environments. - Strong command of JavaScript, React, component architectures, and modern styling approaches. - Good backend fundamentals and ability to work across APIs, databases, and server-side systems - Strong product instincts and attention to UI/UX quality **Why Join:** - Work on genuinely novel AI-native consumer experiences. - High ownership and direct product impact. - Small, fast-moving team with strong engineering and product ambition. - Opportunity to influence both technical architecture and UX direction. **About the Role** We are looking for a skilled MERN Stack Developer – SDE 2 with strong experience in building scalable web applications using MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, and Node.js. The ideal candidate should be comfortable working on both frontend and backend development, writing clean and maintainable code, integrating APIs, handling databases, and taking ownership of features from planning to deployment. **Responsibilities** As an SDE 2 – MERN Stack Developer, you will be responsible for: - Developing and maintaining web applications using the MERN stack. - Building responsive and user-friendly frontend interfaces using React.js. - Creating REST APIs using Node.js and Express.js. - Working with MongoDB for database design, queries, and optimization. - Integrating third-party APIs and services. - Writing clean, reusable, and maintainable code. - Debugging, testing, and fixing application issues. - Collaborating with designers, backend/frontend developers, and project managers. - Participating in code reviews and improving code quality. - Taking ownership of assigned modules and delivering them on time. **Required Skills** - Strong knowledge of JavaScript. - Hands-on experience with React.js. - Experience with Node.js and Express.js. - Good understanding of MongoDB. - Experience in building and consuming REST APIs. - Knowledge of authentication and authorization, such as JWT, cookies, sessions, etc. - Good understanding of HTML, CSS, and responsive design. - Familiarity with Git and GitHub/GitLab. - Ability to write clean and structured code. - Good problem-solving and debugging skills. **Good to Have** - Experience with TypeScript. - Knowledge of Next.js. - Experience with Redux, Zustand, or Context API. - Basic knowledge of cloud deployment such as AWS, Azure, Render, Vercel, or DigitalOcean. - Experience with Docker. - Understanding of CI/CD pipelines. - Knowledge of unit testing or API testing tools like Jest, Postman, or Swagger. **Requirements** - 2–4 years of professional experience in full-stack development. - At least 1.5+ years of hands-on MERN stack experience. - Ability to work independently and take ownership of tasks. - Good communication and teamwork skills. - Strong understanding of frontend and backend development workflows. - Preferred Candidate **We are looking for someone who:** - Can build complete features independently. - Understands both frontend and backend development. - Can debug issues without heavy dependency. - Writes maintainable code. - Is willing to learn and adapt quickly. - Can work in a startup/product-based environment. 6 **Role Overview** We are seeking a Full-Stack Software Developer to join our engineering team and contribute to the design, development and maintenance of modern digital products. The ideal candidate should possess strong software engineering fundamentals, a problem-solving mindset and the ability to build scalable, high-quality applications across web and mobile platforms. This role is suited for individuals who are passionate about technology, product development and delivering reliable software solutions in a fast-paced environment. **Key Responsibilities** The selected candidate will be responsible for developing and maintaining frontend and backend applications, designing and consuming APIs, integrating third-party services and contributing to the overall architecture of software solutions. The role involves participating in the complete software development lifecycle, including requirement analysis, solution design, development, testing, deployment and production support. The candidate will also be expected to contribute to application performance improvements, debugging, issueresolution and implementation of security best practices while ensuring adherence to coding standards and development processes. **Required Skills & Qualifications** - Candidates should have 1–3+ years of experience in software development with strong proficiency in JavaScript, TypeScript, HTML and CSS. - Hands-on experience with React.js, Next.js and Node.js is required. - A solid understanding of RESTful APIs, database design and software engineering principles is essential. - Familiarity with relational and NoSQL databases such as PostgreSQL, MySQL and MongoDB is expected. - The ideal candidate should demonstrate strong analytical thinking, problem-solving capabilities, attention to detail and the ability to write clean, maintainable and scalable code. **Preferred Skills** - Experience with React Native or Flutter, GraphQL, cloud platforms and containerization technologies such as Docker will be considered advantageous. - Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines, automated testing, monitoring tools and modern deployment practices is desirable. Exposure to system design concepts, performance optimization and production support environments will be viewed positively. **Compensation** Compensation will be aligned with industry standards and determined based on experience, technical expertise, project contributions, and interview performance. content.text:
I've read the full 'JD' doc. It actually contains **7 job descriptions** (not 4–5), and they cluster into a clear pattern. Here's a deep analysis to help you prepare.

---

## The 7 roles at a glance

| # | Company type | Core stack | Exp | Distinctive ask |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Aviate/Q-Rate (recruitment SaaS, B2C+B2B) | **Python + React** | 4+ yrs | Scalability, build eng stack from foundation |
| 2 | Internal-tools/dev-productivity | **Python + React/TS** | 2+ yrs | AI agents, ETL automation, daily AI-tool user |
| 3 | thinQit (founding engineer) | **Next.js 14 + React + TS + Node + Azure** | 5+ yrs | Multi-LLM orchestration, MCP, owns whole platform |
| 4 | Fintech (AR/AP, cash forecasting) | **Go + Next.js/React/TS + Postgres + Kafka** | 4+ yrs | Money correctness, API codegen, event-driven |
| 5 | AI-native travel platform | **React-leaning full stack** | not stated | Consumer UX polish, streaming AI interfaces, *portfolio required* |
| 6 | MERN SDE-2 | **MongoDB + Express + React + Node** | 2–4 yrs | Independent feature ownership |
| 7 | General full-stack | **React + Next.js + Node + SQL/NoSQL** | 1–3 yrs | SDLC fundamentals, web + mobile |

---

## What companies are actually looking for

**1. Full-stack ownership is non-negotiable.** Every single JD wants you to own features *end-to-end* — API design → data model → UI → deployment. The phrase "take ownership of features from planning to deployment" or "own features end-to-end" appears in nearly all of them. They don't want a frontend dev who throws work over the wall to backend.

**2. AI-augmented engineering is the new baseline (the biggest shift).** Three of the seven (#2, #3, and #5) treat LLMs/AI agents as core, not bonus:
- Daily use of **Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Codex** is now a *hard requirement*, not a nice-to-have.
- Building **AI agents in production** — tool use, memory, multi-step reasoning, eval pipelines, self-correction loops.
- **Multi-LLM orchestration** (OpenAI + Anthropic + Google), **MCP servers**, model routing for cost/quality/latency tradeoffs.
- This is the highest-leverage thing to learn right now — it separates "2024 full-stack dev" from "2026 full-stack dev."

**3. Startup/founder mentality.** "Comfortable with ambiguity," "ruthless prioritization," "shipping without a safety net," "build complete features independently," "debug without heavy dependency." These are growth/early-stage ventures — they want self-directed builders, not ticket-takers.

**4. Correctness & systems thinking** (especially #3 and #4): transactions, idempotency, careful validation, multi-tenant isolation, async/queue failure modes, observability.

---

## Responsibilities you'd actually get

- **Build & maintain** full-stack web (and sometimes mobile) apps
- **Design & consume REST APIs**, integrate third-party services
- **Database work**: schema design, migrations, query optimization (Postgres + Mongo)
- **Deploy & operate**: CI/CD, Docker, cloud (Azure/AWS), monitoring, incidents, cost optimization
- **Implement security & auth** (JWT, sessions, multi-tenant, data protection)
- **Testing across the stack** (unit + integration + E2E)
- **Collaborate** with product/design/founders; participate in code reviews
- At senior levels (#3): **set architecture, define standards, hire & mentor, direct AI agents**

---

## Most-used tech stack (frequency-ranked)

**Tier 1 — appears almost everywhere (learn deeply):**
- **React.js** — 7/7
- **Node.js / Express** — 5/7
- **TypeScript** — 6/7
- **Next.js** — 5/7
- **PostgreSQL** — 5/7

**Tier 2 — common, expected:**
- **Python** (2 JDs, but as a *primary* language) 
- **MongoDB** (MERN roles)
- **Docker** (5/7)
- **CI/CD — GitHub Actions** (4/7)
- **REST APIs + auth (JWT/sessions)** — universal

**Tier 3 — differentiators / pay-bumps:**
- **LLM APIs (OpenAI/Anthropic/Google), agents, MCP, prompt eng** — increasingly required
- **Go (Golang)** — fintech/high-correctness roles
- **Kafka / message queues** — event-driven systems
- **Azure / AWS, Terraform / Bicep (IaC)**
- **React Native / Expo / Flutter** (mobile)
- **State mgmt** (Redux/Zustand/Context), **GraphQL**, **Playwright/Vitest/Jest**

**The "sweet spot" stack** that unlocks the most of these jobs: **TypeScript + React + Next.js + Node + PostgreSQL + Docker + one AI-tooling competency.** Add **Python** and you cover ~6 of 7.

---

## How to prepare for companies like these

**1. Build 1–2 portfolio projects that prove end-to-end ownership.** This is the single best signal — JD #5 *requires* a portfolio. Don't build a todo app. Build something with: real auth (JWT + multi-tenant), a non-trivial Postgres schema with migrations, REST APIs, a polished React/Next frontend, Docker deployment, and CI/CD. Deploy it live.

**2. Make at least one project AI-native.** This is your biggest differentiator given where the doc is pointing. Build an app with: streaming LLM responses, an **agent that uses tools** (multi-step reasoning), and ideally **multi-model routing** or a simple **MCP server**. Even a small "self-fixing" or "doc-Q&A with citation validation" feature directly mirrors JDs #2 and #3.

**3. Become a fluent AI-coding-tool user — with a philosophy.** JD #2 literally asks for "a clear philosophy on how to apply them." Use Claude Code/Cursor daily, and be able to articulate *where you let agents run and where human judgment is non-negotiable*. Practice driving multiple agents in parallel on a real codebase.

**4. Go deep on fundamentals interviewers probe:**
- **Database**: schema design, indexing, query optimization, transactions, idempotency
- **API design**: REST conventions, contracts/codegen, versioning, error handling
- **Async/systems**: queues, retries, idempotency, failure modes (for the better-paying #3/#4 roles)
- **Auth & security**: JWT vs sessions, OWASP basics, data protection
- **Testing**: unit/integration/E2E discipline (Jest, Vitest, Playwright)

**5. Pick a "depth" specialization to stand out:**
- **AI/agents track** (highest demand in this doc) → LLM orchestration, MCP, evals
- **Correctness track** → Go + Postgres + event-driven systems (fintech #4)
- **UX/consumer track** → React performance, motion/interaction design, streaming UIs (#5)

**6. Practice articulating tradeoffs.** Senior JDs (#3) repeatedly ask you to "make architecture calls and document the *why*." In interviews, narrate cost/quality/latency/speed tradeoffs out loud — that's what they're testing.

**7. Match your resume language to theirs.** Use their exact phrases where true: "shipped to production," "owned end-to-end," "scalable," "multi-tenant," "agentic workflows," "CI/CD ownership."

---

## The single biggest takeaway

The market reflected in this doc has split full-stack into **two tiers**:
- **Baseline (1–4 yrs):** MERN / Next + Node + Postgres, independent feature ownership.
- **Premium (4–5+ yrs):** the same *plus* AI-agent engineering, multi-LLM orchestration, and infra/architecture ownership.

The fastest way to jump tiers — and the clearest signal across these JDs — is **proving you can build and ship production AI-agent features while using AI coding tools to multiply your output.** That's the skill these companies are paying up for in 2026.

---

Want me to turn any of this into something actionable — e.g. a **study/prep roadmap**, a **portfolio project spec** that hits the most JDs at once, or a **resume bullet rewrite** mapped to these keywords? I can also save a profile of your target roles to memory so future prep advice is tailored.
